Unsupervised Cross-lingual Representation Learning at Scale

tailor_summary.core_method: A minimum-viable method assembly for cross-lingual transfer learning in low-resource African languages using XLM-R with candidate modules M1, M2, and M3.
tailor_summary.baseline_model: Unsupervised Cross-lingual Representation Learning at Scale (XLM-R)

tailor_gate.rationale: The tailored method is compatible, falsifiable, and no simpler route is evident given the assembly plan and satisfied evidence gaps.

final_review_gate.rationale: High-severity risks (repetition, differentiation, falsifiability) require targeted re-search to address gaps in baseline comparison, module novelty, and counter-evidence synthesis.

research_package.falsifiable_hypothesis: Adapter layers and knowledge distillation will not significantly improve cross-lingual transfer performance for low-resource African languages compared to vanilla XLM-R Base fine-tuning.

Unsupervised Cross-lingual Representation Learning at Scale

tailor_summary.core_method: A minimum-viable method assembly for cross-lingual transfer learning with XLM-R, incorporating adaptive subsampling and knowledge distillation while excluding retrieval-augmented pretraining due to compatibility constraints.
tailor_summary.baseline_model: Unsupervised Cross-lingual Representation Learning at Scale (XLM-R)

tailor_gate.rationale: Tailored method is compatible (M1/M2 integrated), falsifiable (clear steps), and simpler than M3; no open evidence gaps remain.
